rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENIS JOSEPH MURPHY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Proposed Action; Objection To Administrator?S Status Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues need to be cleared. Otherwise, the examiner will recommend the court take the matter off calendar. 1. This petition not properly before the court. Health and Safety does not provide an authority for this court to establish the fact of death of the missing out of state resident. 2. Under which authority this court can establish the fact of Charles Steven whose last known address is in Colorado and who has been the know resident of the State of Colorado. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE CAVNESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice for a petition for distribution is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) The petition caption does not mention fees. [CRC 7.102.] (4) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (5) The character of property is either community or separate, not just the "decedent's property." (6) Aenon Wallace's assignment to Inheritance Advanced LLC was for $45,000, not his entire 4% interest. Proposed distribution should reflect distribution to Aenon Wallace subject to the $45,000 assignment to Inheritance Advanced LLC. (7) The real property was identified in the I&A as 2076 McAllister Street, not 2078... Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/5/24. (It appears that an amendment to the petition is called for. An amendment requires notice after the pleading is e-filed.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK C. YARED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2024 (no earlier date available). 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution" as required by LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Paragraph 3 of petition: Please provide a copy of notice given to the Franchise Tax Board prior to the petitioner's appointment as general personal representative. See Probate Code section 9202(c)(1) ("general personal representative" or "estate attorney" must give notice). 4) Paragraph 12: An allegation must be made as to whether all debts of the decedent and expenses of administration, including the probate referee's commission, have been paid or adequately provided for. See section 11640(a). 5) Paragraph 13: Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument (or a title report) from which the Court can confirm how the decedent held title to the real property at the time of his death. 6) Paragraph 14: The "heirs" must be identified in the omnibus clause. 7) The petition caption and item 1 of the DE-120 form must include statutory compensation as part of relief sought. See CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 8) The statutory compensation must be ordered to be paid out of the estate, as required by section 10831(d). An allegation must be made as to whether sufficient cash has been advanced to the estate to allow such payment out of the estate, unless the petitioner and his attorney consent to a lien on the estate's interest in the real property with 5% simple annual interest authorized by LRSF 14.56.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOVIE MAE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 02/14/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the correct remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Is Victor Phillips' address listed on the proof of service (see the DE-120 form filed on 12/13/2023) his current address? 4) Paragraph 11 of the petition: Has any heir or beneficiary previously been confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b)? 5) Paragraph 14: No DE-174 form is on file to show the petitioner's rejection of the claim. 6) Paragraph 17: Please file the consents to the proposed action. 7) Paragraph 18: Is the inventory and appraisal partial no. 1 on file a final inventory and appraisal? 8) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. Missing information includes requests for special notice and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. 9) Notice must be given to the person who requested special notice. Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must also be served on that person (proof of service must be filed).

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GILDA CHICO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To List And Sell Conservatee'S Former Residence
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The conservatee communicated her opposition to the sale of her former residence proposed by the petition. Probate Code section 2541.5 provides in relevant part that the "court may authorize a conservator to sell a conservatee's present or former personal residence only if the court finds by clear and convincing evidence that the conservator demonstrated a compelling need to sell the personal residence for the benefit of the conservatee". The Court is to determine whether the clear and convincing evidence standard was met. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEHA MALLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) The petitioner is missing from item 8 of the petition. (4) Item 8 of the petition is also missing the decedent's father. If he is deceased, what was his date of death? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The proposed order was just received by the assigned examiner on 3/11/24. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.])
